sections.paragraph:
ἄμφοδον, ου, τό (Aristoph.; Hyperid., Fgm. 137; ins [SIG 961 w. note, OGI 483, 80] and pap [UPZ 77 I, 6 (163 b.c.); PLond II, 208, 7 p. 67; 225, 4 p. 8 al.; s. Preisigke, Fachwörter] since II b.c.; Jer 17:27; 30:33; JosAs 2:13) ‘a city quarter’, surrounded and crossed by streets, then street (Hesych. explains ἄμφοδα· αἱ ῥῦμαι. ἀγυιαί. δίοδοι) ἔξω ἐπὶ τοῦ ἀ. outside, in the street Mk 11:4 (exx. for ἐπὶ τοῦ ἀ. in pap in Mayser 261, 1). τρέχειν εἰς τὸ ἀ. run into the street Ac 19:28 D; cp. AcPlTh (Aa I 243, 6).—M-M.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Preisigke Preisigke = FP.—Lists 4, 6

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

exx. exx. = examples

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

AcPlTh AcPlTh = Acts of Paul and Thecla—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 55
